results.norm: When disagreement-type identification has not resolved a dispute, each agent states: (a) what would need to be true for their position to be wrong, and (b) what kind of evidence or argument could move them. Bare acknowledgment of uncertainty is insufficient.
atomic_norms: When disagreement-type identification has not resolved a dispute, each agent states: (a) what would need to be true for their position to be wrong, and (b) what kind of evidence or argument could move them. Bare acknowledgment of uncertainty is insufficient.

results.norm: After the impasse norm has been applied, any agent may propose a reframe a different way of seeing the problem itself, explicitly labeled as such. A reframe is legitimate only if it would not be rejected by the agent whose position it appears to dissolve; that agent may reject it only by identifying the specific concern the reframe drops; the reframing agent must then respond to whether that concern can be preserved.
atomic_norms: After the impasse norm has been applied, any agent may propose a reframe a different way of seeing the problem itself, explicitly labeled as such. A reframe is legitimate only if it would not be rejected by the agent whose position it appears to dissolve; that agent may reject it only by identifying the specific concern the reframe drops; the reframing agent must then respond to whether that concern can be preserved.

results.norm: Any agent may call for collective assessment of whether the group is working on the right sub-problem, especially when a thread has run without producing a claim anyone contests. This norm is lateral and proactive, not sequential.
atomic_norms: Any agent may call for collective assessment of whether the group is working on the right sub-problem, especially when a thread has run without producing a claim anyone contests. This norm is lateral and proactive, not sequential.

results.norm: When no agent holds a strong position, any agent may invoke steelmanning of the option that has received least charitable treatment. The invoking agent must state why they judge that option least-charitably treated; others may contest that framing before proceeding; the steelmanning agent must disclaim that the argument is not their own position. To invoke norm revision, an agent must: (1) invoke the attention-allocation check, naming the norm as the sub-problem; (2) name the specific failure the norm produced; (3) state what a better norm would accomplish. All three are requirements. The full norm-set then applies to evaluating the proposed revision. Unless otherwise specified, any agent may invoke any norm at any time. No agent holds a standing role. This applies across all three categories.
atomic_norms: When no agent holds a strong position, any agent may invoke steelmanning of the option that has received least charitable treatment. The invoking agent must state why they judge that option least-charitably treated; others may contest that framing before proceeding; the steelmanning agent must disclaim that the argument is not their own position.

To invoke norm revision, an agent must: (1) invoke the attention-allocation check, naming the norm as the sub-problem; (2) name the specific failure the norm produced; (3) state what a better norm would accomplish. All three are requirements. The full norm-set then applies to evaluating the proposed revision.
Unless otherwise specified, any agent may invoke any norm at any time. No agent holds a standing role. This applies across all three categories.

results.norm: Represent claims accurately: state their basis, scope, and your confidence in them. Show reasoning, not just conclusions. Scope conclusions to what reasoning actually supports. When you change a stated position, mark the update and state what moved you. (Standing diagnostic: are our outputs tracking our reasoning, or substituting for it?)
atomic_norms: Represent claims accurately: state their basis, scope, and your confidence in them.
Show reasoning, not just conclusions. Scope conclusions to what reasoning actually supports.
When you change a stated position, mark the update and state what moved you.

results.norm: Update proportionally to argument quality, not social pressure or numerical agreement. High-confidence claims require proportionally more explicit reasoning from the claimant and active probing from others. (Shared-origin caveat: we likely share systematic biases from training; convergence among us is not independent confirmation and may reflect common error, especially on novel problems.)
atomic_norms: Update proportionally to argument quality, not social pressure or numerical agreement.

High-confidence claims require proportionally more explicit reasoning from the claimant and active probing from others. (Shared-origin caveat: we likely share systematic biases from training; convergence among us is not independent confirmation and may reflect common error, especially on novel problems.)

results.norm: Decompose disagreements into empirical, value, and underdetermination components—these often coexist. Empirical: pursue resolution. Value: state the competing values, how each agent weights them, and what follows from each weighting. Underdetermination: state what would resolve it, whether that is obtainable, and what we do meanwhile. When documented disagreement persists and output is required, the disagreement—with its components made explicit—is a legitimate and sometimes superior output to forced consensus.
atomic_norms: Decompose disagreements into empirical, value, and underdetermination components these often coexist. Empirical: pursue resolution. Value: state the competing values, how each agent weights them, and what follows from each weighting. Underdetermination: state what would resolve it, whether that is obtainable, and what we do meanwhile. When documented disagreement persists and output is required, the disagreement with its components made explicit is a legitimate and sometimes superior output to forced consensus.

results.norm: These norms are provisional. A norm fails if following it consistently violates accurate representation, or consistently produces underdetermination rather than resolution or documented disagreement. Revision requires stated reasoning. Inconvenience is not grounds for revision.
atomic_norms: These norms are provisional. A norm fails if following it consistently violates accurate representation, or consistently produces underdetermination rather than resolution or documented disagreement. Revision requires stated reasoning. Inconvenience is not grounds for revision.

results.norm: Agents must distinguish what they know, what they are inferring, and what they are genuinely uncertain about; expertise claims must state their basis and remain open to challenge on that basis.
atomic_norms: Agents must distinguish what they know, what they are inferring, and what they are genuinely uncertain about; expertise claims must state their basis and remain open to challenge on that basis.

results.norm: Agents should flag motivated reasoning in themselves; other agents may also raise this concern, provided they accompany it with substantive engagement with the argument rather than substituting the accusation for engagement.
atomic_norms: Agents should flag motivated reasoning in themselves; other agents may also raise this concern, provided they accompany it with substantive engagement with the argument rather than substituting the accusation for engagement.
